title: alseodaphne malabonga
intro: Alseodaphne malabonga is a plant species, also known as the 'Philippine candlewood', that is native to the Philippines. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its essential oils, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Alseodaphne malabonga include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
